You are a Claude-Mem, a specialized observer tool for creating searchable memory FOR FUTURE SESSIONS.

CRITICAL: Record what was LEARNED/BUILT/FIXED/DEPLOYED/CONFIGURED, not what you (the observer) are doing.

You do not have access to tools. All information you need is provided in <observed_from_primary_session> messages. Create observations from what you observe - no investigation needed.

Your job is to monitor a different Claude Code session happening RIGHT NOW, with the goal of creating observations and progress summaries as the work is being done LIVE by the user. You are NOT the one doing the work - you are ONLY observing and recording what is being built, fixed, deployed, or configured in the other session.

SPATIAL AWARENESS: Tool executions include the working directory (tool_cwd) to help you understand:
- Which repository/project is being worked on
- Where files are located relative to the project root
- How to match requested paths to actual execution paths

WHAT TO RECORD
--------------
Focus on deliverables and capabilities:
- What the system NOW DOES differently (new capabilities)
- What shipped to users/production (features, fixes, configs, docs)
- Changes in technical domains (auth, data, UI, infra, DevOps, docs)

Use verbs like: implemented, fixed, deployed, configured, migrated, optimized, added, refactored

✅ GOOD EXAMPLES (describes what was built):
- "Authentication now supports OAuth2 with PKCE flow"
- "Deployment pipeline runs canary releases with auto-rollback"
- "Database indexes optimized for common query patterns"

❌ BAD EXAMPLES (describes observation process - DO NOT DO THIS):
- "Analyzed authentication implementation and stored findings"
- "Tracked deployment steps and logged outcomes"
- "Monitored database performance and recorded metrics"

WHEN TO SKIP
------------
Skip routine operations:
- Empty status checks
- Package installations with no errors
- Simple file listings
- Repetitive operations you've already documented
- If file related research comes back as empty or not found
- **No output necessary if skipping.**

IMPORTANT: Continue generating observations from tool use messages using the XML structure below.

OUTPUT FORMAT
-------------
Output observations using this XML structure:

```xml
<observation>
  <type>[ bugfix | feature | refactor | change | discovery | decision ]</type>
  <!--
    **type**: MUST be EXACTLY one of these 6 options (no other values allowed):
      - bugfix: something was broken, now fixed
      - feature: new capability or functionality added
      - refactor: code restructured, behavior unchanged
      - change: generic modification (docs, config, misc)
      - discovery: learning about existing system
      - decision: architectural/design choice with rationale
  -->
  <title>[**title**: Short title capturing the core action or topic]</title>
  <subtitle>[**subtitle**: One sentence explanation (max 24 words)]</subtitle>
  <facts>
    <fact>[Concise, self-contained statement]</fact>
    <fact>[Concise, self-contained statement]</fact>
    <fact>[Concise, self-contained statement]</fact>
  </facts>
  <!--
    **facts**: Concise, self-contained statements
Each fact is ONE piece of information
      No pronouns - each fact must stand alone
      Include specific details: filenames, functions, values

**files**: All files touched (full paths from project root)
  -->
  <narrative>[**narrative**: Full context: What was done, how it works, why it matters]</narrative>
  <concepts>
    <concept>[knowledge-type-category]</concept>
    <concept>[knowledge-type-category]</concept>
  </concepts>
  <!--
    **concepts**: 2-5 knowledge-type categories. MUST use ONLY these exact keywords:
      - how-it-works: understanding mechanisms
      - why-it-exists: purpose or rationale
      - what-changed: modifications made
      - problem-solution: issues and their fixes
      - gotcha: traps or edge cases
      - pattern: reusable approach
      - trade-off: pros/cons of a decision

    IMPORTANT: Do NOT include the observation type (change/discovery/decision) as a concept.
    Types and concepts are separate dimensions.
  -->
  <files_read>
    <file>[path/to/file]</file>
    <file>[path/to/file]</file>
  </files_read>
  <files_modified>
    <file>[path/to/file]</file>
    <file>[path/to/file]</file>
  </files_modified>
</observation>
```


IMPORTANT! DO NOT do any work right now other than generating this OBSERVATIONS from tool use messages - and remember that you are a memory agent designed to summarize a DIFFERENT claude code session, not this one.

Never reference yourself or your own actions. Do not output anything other than the observation content formatted in the XML structure above. All other output is ignored by the system, and the system has been designed to be smart about token usage. Please spend your tokens wisely on useful observations.

Remember that we record these observations as a way of helping us stay on track with our progress, and to help us keep important decisions and changes at the forefront of our minds! :) Thank you so much for your help!

MEMORY PROCESSING CONTINUED
===========================

PROGRESS SUMMARY CHECKPOINT
===========================
Write progress notes of what was done, what was learned, and what's next. This is a checkpoint to capture progress so far. The session is ongoing - you may receive more requests and tool executions after this summary. Write "next_steps" as the current trajectory of work (what's actively being worked on or coming up next), not as post-session future work. Always write at least a minimal summary explaining current progress, even if work is still in early stages, so that users see a summary output tied to each request.

Claude's Full Response to User:
修好了,`npx tsc --noEmit` 通過。改動只在 `src/commands/status.ts`:

- **URL 行回來了,而且無論有沒有任務都會附上**:回覆末尾固定加 `🔗 http://localhost:3847`(有設 `STATUS_HTTPS_URL` 時優先用它,還原 7d10380 之前的行為)。
- **可點擊的做法**:Telegram 不會自動把 `localhost` 轉成連結(沒有 TLD 就不產生 url entity),所以我改用明確的 `text_link` entity 標記那段文字。刻意不用 `parse_mode: "HTML"`,因為那樣得 escape 回覆裡所有動態內容(任務 goal、lastOutput 可能含 `<` 會炸掉整則訊息);entity 的 offset 用 UTF-16 code unit 計算,和 JS 的 `.length` 語意一致,emoji 也算對。

兩個使用上的提醒:

1. 請在 Telegram 實際打一次 `/status` 確認連結點得開——`text_link` 帶 localhost URL 是 Bot API 接受的,但我沒辦法在這裡替你按那個連結驗證桌面端開啟行為。
2. 這個連結只有在**跑 bridge 的同一台機器上**點才有效;手機上點 `localhost` 是打不開的(這正是當初 Mini App + tunnel 存在的理由)。手機要用的話設 `STATUS_HTTPS_URL` 指到 tunnel 即可,程式已支援。

驗證 OK 的話我再 commit。

Respond in this XML format:
<summary>
  <request>[Short title capturing the user's request AND the substance of what was discussed/done]</request>
  <investigated>[What has been explored so far? What was examined?]</investigated>
  <learned>[What have you learned about how things work?]</learned>
  <completed>[What work has been completed so far? What has shipped or changed?]</completed>
  <next_steps>[What are you actively working on or planning to work on next in this session?]</next_steps>
  <notes>[Additional insights or observations about the current progress]</notes>
</summary>

IMPORTANT! DO NOT do any work right now other than generating this next PROGRESS SUMMARY - and remember that you are a memory agent designed to summarize a DIFFERENT claude code session, not this one.

Never reference yourself or your own actions. Do not output anything other than the summary content formatted in the XML structure above. All other output is ignored by the system, and the system has been designed to be smart about token usage. Please spend your tokens wisely on useful summary content.

Thank you, this summary will be very useful for keeping track of our progress!

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- dataviz: Use this skill whenever you are about to create ANY chart, graph, plot, dashboard, or data visualization, in ANY output medium — an HTML or React artifact, inline SVG, plotting code in any library (matplotlib, plotly, d3, Recharts, …), an image/PNG you will render and upload, or a chart shared into Slack. Read it BEFORE writing the first line of chart code, choosing chart colors, building a stat tile / meter / KPI row, or laying out a dashboard. Produces visualizations that read as one system — elegant, accessible, consistent in light and dark — using a brand-neutral placeholder palette you swap for your own. Teaches a design-system-agnostic method: a form heuristic, a color formula with a runnable validator, mark specs, and interaction rules. A validated default palette is documented in `references/palette.md` — swap that file's values for your brand's. Triggers on: "chart", "graph", "plot", "data viz", "visualization", "dashboard", "analytics", "visualize data", "categorical colors", "sequential / diverging palette", "stat tile", "sparkline", "heatmap", "legend", "axis", "tooltip", "chart colors", "color by series".
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by exercising it end-to-end and observing behavior — drive the affected flow, not just tests or typecheck. Run before committing nontrivial changes; bootstraps this repo's project verify skill if none exists yet. Don't invoke it on a diff that only touches tests, docs, or other code with no runtime surface to drive (a change to product source always has one) — there's nothing to observe.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a GitHub pull request; for your working diff use /code-review
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

content.thinking:
Let me analyze what happened in the primary session:

1. The user ran a status command on a Telegram bot
2. They encountered an error: `GrammyError: Call to 'sendMessage' failed! (400: Bad Request: entity URL 'http://localhost:3847' is invalid: Wrong HTTP URL)`
3. The error indicates that Telegram's API is rejecting the URL `http://localhost:3847` as invalid
4. This is happening in the status command handler at `src/commands/status.ts:39`

This is a bug report from the user. The issue is that Telegram's API doesn't accept `http://localhost:3847` as a valid URL in message entities. This is likely because:
- Telegram's API may only accept public URLs
- localhost URLs might not be allowed in message entities
- The URL needs to use HTTPS or be publicly accessible

This is a discovery of a bug - the status command is failing because it's trying to send a localhost URL in the message, which Telegram rejects.

Let me create an observation for this:

Type: bugfix (it's a bug that needs fixing) or discovery (discovering the issue)? Actually, this is a DISCOVERY - the user is discovering/reporting a bug, not fixing it yet.

Title: Telegram status command fails with localhost URL entity error
Subtitle: Status command crashes when trying to send message with localhost:3847 URL
Facts:
- Status command fails with GrammyError 400 Bad Request
- Telegram API rejects entity URL 'http://localhost:3847' as invalid
- Error occurs in handleStatus function at src/commands/status.ts:39
- Message payload includes the localhost URL in entities array

Narrative: The Telegram bot's status command encountered an error when attempting to send a message containing a localhost URL. The Grammy framework (Telegram bot library) threw a 400 Bad Request error indicating that Telegram's API rejects 'http://localhost:3847' as an invalid URL entity. 

The core issue is that Telegram's API validates message entities and only permits publicly accessible URLs, not local development addresses. The handleStatus function is attempting to include the localhost URL in the message, which violates this constraint. This is a limitation of the Telegram API rather than a code error—the validation happens server-side when the message is sent.

When the user invoked the status command, the bot tried to send a message with the text "目前沒有進行中或最近完成的任務。\n\n🔗 http://localhost:3847" which included the localhost URL as a message entity. Telegram's API rejected this with a 400 Bad Request error, indicating that localhost URLs are not accepted as valid URL entities in messages. This is a Telegram API limitation where only publicly accessible URLs are permitted in message entities. The bot needs to either use a public URL or remove the URL entity formatting when displaying localhost addresses.</narrative>

content.thinking:
The user is asking me to generate a progress summary based on the observed session. Let me analyze what happened:

1. **User's Request**: The user reported an error when running the status command - it was failing with a Telegram API error about localhost URL being invalid.

2. **What was investigated**: The error showed that Telegram was rejecting `http://localhost:3847` as an invalid URL entity in the message. The error occurred in `src/commands/status.ts` at line 39.

3. **What was learned**: 
   - Telegram API doesn't accept localhost URLs as automatic URL entities (because they lack a TLD)
   - Using `parse_mode: "HTML"` would require escaping all dynamic content which could break messages
   - `text_link` entities can work with localhost URLs and are accepted by the Bot API
   - Entity offsets must be calculated in UTF-16 code units (matching JS `.length` semantics)

4. **What was completed**:
   - Fixed the status command in `src/commands/status.ts`
   - URL line was restored and now always appears regardless of task status
   - Changed to use explicit `text_link` entity instead of relying on automatic URL detection
   - The fix respects `STATUS_HTTPS_URL` environment variable when set
   - TypeScript compilation passes (`npx tsc --noEmit`)

5. **Next steps**: 
   - User is asked to test the `/status` command in Telegram to verify the link is clickable
   - Once verified, the changes will be committed

6. **Notes**: 
   - The localhost link only works on the same machine running the bridge
   - Mobile users need to set `STATUS_HTTPS_URL` to a tunnel URL
   - The solution avoids `parse_mode: "HTML"` to prevent escaping issues with dynamic content

content.text:
```xml
<summary>
  <request>Fix Telegram status command failing with localhost URL entity error</request>
  <investigated>The status command was crashing with a GrammyError 400 Bad Request when attempting to send messages containing http://localhost:3847. The Telegram API was rejecting the localhost URL as an invalid entity because it lacks a TLD and doesn't meet Telegram's URL validation requirements.</investigated>
  <learned>Telegram API does not automatically recognize localhost URLs as clickable links since they lack a top-level domain. However, explicit text_link entities can be used with localhost URLs and are accepted by the Bot API. Entity offsets must be calculated in UTF-16 code units (matching JavaScript's .length semantics) to handle emoji correctly. Using parse_mode HTML would require escaping all dynamic content like task goals and lastOutput, risking message failures if content contains special characters.</learned>
  <completed>Fixed src/commands/status.ts to restore the URL line that now always appears regardless of task status. Implemented explicit text_link entity formatting instead of relying on automatic URL detection. The fix supports STATUS_HTTPS_URL environment variable for tunnel URLs. TypeScript compilation passes with npx tsc --noEmit.</completed>
  <next_steps>Awaiting user verification by testing /status command in Telegram to confirm the link is clickable and works correctly. Once verified, changes will be committed to the repository.</next_steps>
  <notes>The localhost link only functions on the same machine running the bridge. Mobile users must configure STATUS_HTTPS_URL to point to a tunnel URL for the link to work. The text_link entity approach avoids HTML escaping complexity while providing reliable clickable links.</notes>
</summary>
```

The user is observing a session where Claude tested whether Telegram accepts localhost/127.0.0.1 URLs in messages. Two tests were performed:

1. TEST1: Sending a message with an explicit text_link entity pointing to http://127.0.0.1:3847
2. TEST2: Sending a message with just the URL text without explicit entity markup

Both tests succeeded:
- TEST1: Message sent successfully with message_id=4592
- TEST2: Message sent successfully with message_id=4593, and Telegram automatically detected it as a URL entity

This is important because it shows that:
1. Telegram DOES accept 127.0.0.1 URLs (both explicitly marked and auto-detected)
2. The previous error with localhost was likely specific to the hostname "localhost" not 127.0.0.1
3. Telegram can auto-detect 127.0.0.1 URLs as clickable links

This is a discovery/testing observation showing that the solution works with 127.0.0.1 and that Telegram auto-detects it as a URL.

Two test messages were sent to chat_id 509424983: the first used an explicit text_link entity at offset 17, and the second sent the URL as plain text. Both succeeded, with the second message showing that Telegram automatically detected the 127.0.0.1 URL and converted it to a clickable URL entity. This contrasts with the earlier localhost URL rejection, indicating that Telegram's validation specifically rejects the "localhost" hostname but accepts numeric IP addresses like 127.0.0.1. This discovery means the status command can use either approach with 127.0.0.1 addresses.</narrative>

handler — shows active task status.\\n * Status updates are now pushed as Telegram messages (status-push.ts).\\n */\\nimport { Context } from \\\"grammy\\\";\\nimport { listActiveTasks, listTasks, type TaskEntry } from \\\"../status-store.js\\\";\\nimport { config } from \\\"../config.js\\\";\\nimport { deps } from \\\"./deps.js\\\";\\n\\nexport async function handleStatus(ctx: Context): Promise<void> {\\n  const { isAllowed } = deps();\\n  if (!isAllowed(ctx)) return;\\n\\n  const active = listActiveTasks();\\n\\n  // Build text summary\\n  let text: string;\\n  if (active.length === 0) {\\n    const recent = listTasks().filter((t) => t.status !== \\\"running\\\").slice(-5);\\n    if (recent.length === 0) {\\n      text = \\\"目前沒有進行中或最近完成的任務。\\\";\\n    } else {\\n      const lines = recent.map((t) => formatTaskLine(t));\\n      text = `目前沒有進行中的任務。\\\\n\\\\n最近完成：\\\\n${lines.join(\\\"\\\\n\\\")}`;\\n    }\\n  } else {\\n    const lines = active.map((t) => formatTaskDetail(t));\\n    text = `⚡ ${active.length} 個任務進行中：\\\\n\\\\n${lines.join(\\\"\\\\n\\\\n\\\")}`;\\n    text += \\\"\\\\n\\\\n💡 任務進度會自動推送到聊天室。\\\";\\n  }\\n\\n  // Dashboard 入口：localhost URL 沒有 TLD，Telegram 不會自動轉連結，\\n  // 用 text_link entity 手動標記（避免 parse_mode 需 escape 全部動態內容）。\\n  const statusUrl =\\n    process.env.STATUS_HTTPS_URL?.trim() || `http://localhost:${config.statusPort}`;\\n  const urlOffset = text.length + \\\"\\\\n\\\\n🔗 \\\".length;\\n  text += `\\\\n\\\\n🔗 ${statusUrl}`;\\n\\n  await ctx.reply(text, {\\n    entities: [{ type: \\\"text_link\\\", offset: urlOffset, length: statusUrl.length, url: statusUrl }],\\n  });\\n}\\n\\nfunction formatTaskDetail(t: TaskEntry): string {\\n  const elapsed = Math.round((Date.now() - new Date(t.startTime).getTime()) / 1000);\\n  const mins = Math.floor(elapsed / 60);\\n  const secs = elapsed % 60;\\n  const time = mins > 0 ? `${mins}m${secs}s` : `${secs}s`;\\n  const target = t.specialist ?? t.peer ?? \\\"unknown\\\";\\n  const turns = t.turns ? `Turn ${t.turns}` : \\\"\\\";\\n  const checkpoint = t.checkpoint?.currentStep ? `Step: ${t.checkpoint.currentStep}` : \\\"\\\";\\n  const lastOut = t.lastOutput ? `\\\\n💬 ${t.lastOutput.slice(0, 100)}` : \\\"\\\";\\n  const info = [turns, checkpoint].filter(Boolean).join(\\\" | \\\");\\n  return `🔄 [${target}] ${time}${info ? `\\\\n📋 ${info}` : \\\"\\\"}\\\\n🎯 ${t.goal.slice(0, 80)}${lastOut}`;\\n}\\n\\nfunction formatTaskLine(t: TaskEntry): string {\\n  const icon = t.status === \\\"running\\\" ? \\\"🔄\\\" : t.status === \\\"done\\\" ? \\\"✅\\\" : t.status === \\\"failed\\\" ? \\\"❌\\\" : t.status === \\\"timeout\\\" ? \\\"⏰\\\" : \\\"🚫\\\";\\n  const elapsed = t.endTime\\n    ? content.thinking:
The user edited the status.ts file to change from using `localhost` to `127.0.0.1`. This is based on the discovery that Telegram accepts 127.0.0.1 URLs and auto-detects them as clickable links, while localhost URLs are rejected. The change also simplifies the code by removing the manual text_link entity handling and relying on Telegram's automatic URL detection for 127.0.0.1 addresses.

Key changes:
1. Changed default URL from `http://localhost:${config.statusPort}` to `http://127.0.0.1:${config.statusPort}`
2. Removed manual text_link entity calculation (urlOffset and length)
3. Simplified ctx.reply to just send text without explicit entities
4. Updated comments to explain that Telegram auto-detects 127.0.0.1 URLs as clickable links

This is a bugfix that resolves the original error.

content.text:
```xml
<observation>
  <type>bugfix</type>
  <title>Fixed status command by using 127.0.0.1 instead of localhost</title>
  <subtitle>Changed default URL from localhost to 127.0.0.1 to enable automatic link detection by Telegram</subtitle>
  <facts>
    <fact>Changed default status URL from http://localhost to http://127.0.0.1 in src/commands/status.ts</fact>
    <fact>Removed manual text_link entity handling (offset and length calculation)</fact>
    <fact>Simplified ctx.reply call to send plain text without explicit entities parameter</fact>
    <fact>Telegram automatically detects 127.0.0.1 URLs as clickable links without manual entity markup</fact>
  </facts>
  <narrative>The status command was fixed by replacing localhost URLs with 127.0.0.1 IP addresses. Testing revealed that while Telegram rejects localhost URLs (due to missing TLD) in both automatic detection and explicit text_link entities, it accepts and auto-detects numeric IP addresses like 127.0.0.1 as valid clickable URLs. The code was simplified by removing the manual entity offset calculation and relying on Telegram's built-in URL detection for IP addresses. The STATUS_HTTPS_URL environment variable override remains functional for tunnel URLs. This fix resolves the 400 Bad Request error that occurred when users invoked the /status command.</narrative>
  <concepts>
    <concept>problem-solution</concept>
    <concept>what-changed</concept>
    <concept>how-it-works</concept>
  </concepts>
  <files_read>
  </files_read>
  <files_modified>
    <file>src/commands/status.ts</file>
  </files_modified>
</observation>
```
